sound_type: wing claps + soft clucking -- short flutter flights with audible wing claps, accompanied by soft clucking contact calls; E=4
ecosystem: Boreal spruce forest -- dense conifer forest dominated by Engelmann spruce, Sitka spruce, subalpine fir. Dark, quiet, deep needle duff. Most habitat-specific grouse in North America.
season: Year-round resident. Wing clap display April-June. Non-migratory (unlike Dusky/Sooty reverse migration).
family: Phasianidae (5th entry. Extends beyond the quartet 153-156.)

coordinates.description: Spruce Grouse in boreal spruce forests of northeastern Washington -- Okanogan Highlands, Selkirk Mountains, northern Cascades higher elevations.

taxonomy_note.description: FIFTH Phasianidae entry. Extends beyond the quartet. The 'fool hen' -- legendary tameness. Boreal spruce forest specialist. Wing clap display + soft clucking. Quietest Phasianidae entry (E=4).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Phasianidae: fifth entry. Wild Turkey (153) + Dusky Grouse (154) + Ruffed Grouse (155) + Sooty Grouse (156) + Spruce Grouse (157). Quartet extended.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Falcipennis from Latin 'falx' (sickle) + 'penna' (feather) -- referring to the curved outer primaries. canadensis -- of Canada, reflecting the species' boreal range.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Falcipennis -- first and only entry. Monotypic genus in the engine. Distinguished from Dendragapus (Dusky/Sooty) and Bonasa (Ruffed) by wing shape and boreal habitat.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fifth Phasianidae. Beyond the quartet. Quietest entry. The coda.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Wing claps: broadband mechanical, 200-2000 Hz. Clucking: 500-2000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Wing claps: broadband mechanical. Clucking: ~500-2000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Wing clap flight: 1-2 seconds. Clucking: individual clucks ~0.1s, series variable.
song_metrics.note_structure: WING CLAPS: Mechanical sound from rapid wing beats during short flutter flight. Dry, sharp, percussive. SOFT CLUCKING: Quiet repetitive cluck-cluck-cluck. Intimate.
song_metrics.note_types: Wing claps (primary display -- mechanical), soft clucking (contact/courtship -- vocal), hissing (alarm -- vocal).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Wing clap: single burst during 1-2 second flutter flight. May repeat multiple flights. Clucking: series of quiet clucks.
song_metrics.timbre: Wing claps: dry, crackling, percussive. Clucking: soft, intimate, low-amplitude. Overall: the most understated acoustic profile of any Phasianidae.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Wing claps audible 30-50 meters. Clucking audible 10-20 meters. Short-range. Intimate.
song_metrics.tempo: Wing clap flights intermittent. Clucking: moderate repetition rate.
song_metrics.context: Breeding season (April-June). Male territorial/courtship display. Wing claps combined with ground strutting and tail-fan display.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Slight variation across range. Washington birds at southern edge of range.
song_metrics.key_property: WING CLAPS + SOFT CLUCKING -- quiet, intimate, understated. The 'fool hen.' E=4. FIFTH PHASIANIDAE. Beyond the quartet. Quietest entry. Boreal spruce specialist.

plumage.overall: Males: dark gray-brown with fine white barring. Red eyebrow combs (prominent during display). Females: heavily barred brown, cryptic. Both sexes darker and more uniformly colored than Ruffed Grouse.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Males: red eyebrow combs, dark plumage with white-tipped undertail coverts. Short, rounded tail with rufous tip. Females: barred brown, smaller than Ruffed Grouse.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Red eyebrow combs enlarge during breeding display. Otherwise minimal seasonal change.
plumage.flight_pattern: Burst flight when flushed. Short distance. Often reluctant to flush at all (fool hen behavior).

physical_description.overall: Length: 38-43 cm. Weight: 450-650 g. Medium grouse. Smaller than Dusky/Sooty Grouse. Compact build.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Dark plumage, red eyebrow combs, rufous tail tip. Boreal habitat. Extreme tameness.
physical_description.field_id: Dark grouse in dense spruce forest that may not fly when approached. Listen for soft clucking and occasional wing clap flights. The tameness is diagnostic.

nodes.title: Wing Clap Display -- Brief Percussive Flights
nodes.description: Short flutter flights (1-2 meters up) with audible wing claps. Brief, percussive, 30-50 meter range. Combined with ground strutting and tail-fan display. Mechanical sound similar to but quieter than Rock Pigeon wing clap (152).

nodes.title: Fool Hen -- Legendary Tameness
nodes.description: So tame it can be approached by hand. Predator naivety from cryptic-plumage strategy. Low human contact in vast boreal range. Sedentary conifer-needle foraging. Historical accounts of hand-catching.

nodes.title: Phasianidae Extension (153-157)
nodes.description: Fifth Phasianidae entry. Beyond the quartet (153-156). Quietest entry (E=4 after E=6, E=5, E=5, E=5). Loudest to most mysterious to quietest. The coda that completes the family arc.

nodes.title: Boreal Forest Specialist
nodes.description: Dense spruce forest. Conifer needle diet (spruce needles primary). Year-round resident. Enlarged cecum for cellulose digestion. Most habitat-specific grouse in North America. Quiet forest = quiet bird.

cross_references.relationship: QUARTET + CODA! Sooty Grouse (156, ventriloquial, E=5) closed the quartet. Spruce Grouse (157, quiet, E=4) extends as coda. Mystery to silence.
MECHANICAL COMPARISON! Ruffed Grouse drumming (155, explosive, E=5) vs. Spruce Grouse wing claps (157, quiet, E=4). Both mechanical wing sounds at vastly different intensities.
WING CLAP ECHO! Rock Pigeon wing clap (152) and Spruce Grouse wing claps (157). Both mechanical wing sounds in display contexts. Urban vs. boreal.
PHASIANIDAE RANGE! Turkey (153, E=6, loudest) to Spruce Grouse (157, E=4, quietest). Maximum energy range within one family across five entries.
